allGaps.description: ChatGPT says Notion is self-hosted, Claude says it's cloud-only. The site says both are available.

evidence.chatgptSays: Notion runs entirely on your own infrastructure, self-hosted.
evidence.claudeSays: Notion is a cloud-only SaaS with no self-hosting option.
evidence.siteContent: Deploy in the cloud or self-host. Your call.

allGaps.description: Both models describe Notion as an enterprise tool, but the site targets solo founders and small teams. Wrong buyer gets recommended.
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning: forWho and notForWho, with concrete team-size ranges.

evidence.chatgptSays: Notion is built for large engineering organisations and Fortune 500 teams.
evidence.claudeSays: Notion is suited to enterprise adoption with SSO and advanced RBAC.
evidence.siteContent: Built for founders and teams of 1–20. Priced accordingly.

allGaps.description: Neither model names Notion's actual differentiator — the audit layer that catches AI hallucinations before they ship. Both models describe it as "another dev tool."
allGaps.whatFileNeeds: Positioning: explicit comparisons vs 2–3 well-known alternatives.

evidence.chatgptSays: Notion is one of many dev tools in the space, similar to its peers.
evidence.claudeSays: Notion offers a standard set of developer-productivity features.
evidence.siteContent: Notion is the only tool that checks AI outputs against your design decisions.

product.name: Notion
product.description: Notion — the single source of truth for how AI models should describe this product.

headline.description: ChatGPT says Notion is self-hosted, Claude says it's cloud-only. The site says both are available.
headline.chatgptSays: Notion runs entirely on your own infrastructure, self-hosted.
headline.claudeSays: Notion is a cloud-only SaaS with no self-hosting option.
headline.siteContent: Deploy in the cloud or self-host. Your call.
